transcript.clean_text: The Tour de Suisse is deep into the mountains and you can watch it each and every day live and on demand on GCN+ Day four of the 86th edition of the event would begin in Monte heading up the valley towards Sion. Martigny first, the Roman city in Switzerland, and then a climb to Crans-Montana. No stage finish this time as they had been at the Giro d'Italia. Instead, a descent downhill climbing yet again, climbing in fact all the way to the finish. Couple of short descents on the way but mainly uphill to Leukerbad. Another difficult day with all of the climbing back loaded, 152.5 K's in total. After the first hour was ridden at almost 54 km/h down in the valley, 10 riders got up the road and then that changed with Gino Mäder making it across to make it 11. He, however, just under 2 minutes down in the general classification was a danger to those behind. Trek-Segafredo seemed pretty happy though to let things play out. They kept the gap at just over 2 minutes as Pascal Eenkhoorn beat Lilian Calmejane to maximum King of the Mountains points, not once but twice. Calmejane, however, would move into the lead in the King of the Mountains competition. On to the final climb of the day. The first half of it being the hardest part of it and things started to shake up. Not just in the breakaway but in the peloton, too. Michael Schär leading out Felix Gall to attack from the main bunch. Juan Ayuso started to go backwards. But Gall was very much going forwards, helped out by Stan Dewulf who'd been in the break as well. And when he reached the front, he wouldn't waste his time in leaving them behind. Gall looking for his first ever pro bike race win, still with great legs today after he'd come second at the top in Villars-sur-Ollon. He took the lead and Romain Bardet started to attack from the group of favorites. Gall's lead about a minute with Bardet on the march. But others would soon try to join him. The yellow jersey group being whittled down rather seriously. Kelderman would be the next to have a go. Bilbao and Oier Lazkano would also try their own luck. And Evenepoel alongside a couple others including Uran would be distanced. That was only brief though. Evenepoel coming back several times showing that maybe what he was lacking in legs, he was not lacking in fighting spirit. Gall would continue extend his lead moving into the virtual race lead at one point having started the day at 1 minute and 7 seconds down. And as they looked around at each other behind Gall would have an opportunity to not just ride away for the win but look for that yellow jersey. Bardet attacked in the final kilometer yet again. Most aggressive we've seen the Frenchman for quite a while but it was a ride of his life for Felix Gall. So many second place in the last couple of months, finally a first pro win and then he started the clock to those chasing him. Evenepoel after being dropped several times and recovering would fight back to take second place. Six bonus seconds would go with it. And Mattias Skjelmose making sure he finished in third, four bonus seconds for him. But as the clock ticked up, the maths would needed to be done rather quickly. And once the results of the sums were in, Felix Gall won by 1 minute and 2, 1:03 to Skjelmose with a bonus seconds meaning that Gall took not just the stage win but also the yellow jersey by just 2 seconds. New GC looking like this, 2 seconds back to Skjelmose, 16 to Evenepoel, Bilbao now fourth at 57, Kelderman in fifth at 1:12 with Ayuso dropping to sixth. A day of dreams for Austrian rider Felix Gall, the 25-year-old taking the stage win yellow jersey and the white jersey, whereas Lilian Calmejane just about pulled on the King of the Mountains jersey with Gall just behind two points further back. A good day for the Frenchman, two successive days in the breakaway. Day five though will be a tough one for anybody wherever they're riding it. Three giant passes to be taken on finishing with the Albulapass and a long descent down into La Punt. It's 211 km in total two world category climbs, a first category climb, the queen stage of this year's Tour de Suisse. Stage five will start with a new yellow jersey. You can find out if Felix Gall can hold on or if there'll be another name at the top after the fifth day of racing by watching GCN+.
